content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA of size NNN.

You can rearrange the elements of AAA as you want. After that, construct an array BBB of size NNN as:


B1=A1B_1 = A_1B1​=A1​;
Bi=Bi−1B_i = B_{i - 1}Bi​=Bi−1​ &\&& AiA_iAi​ for all 2≤i≤N2 \le i \le N2≤i≤N, where &\&& denotes the bitwise AND operator.
Find the **lexicographically largest** possible array BBB.

For two arrays XXX and YYY, both of size NNN, the array XXX is said to be lexicographically larger than array YYY, if, in the first position where XXX and YYY differ, Xi>YiX_i \gt Y_iXi​>Yi​.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains NNN, the size of the array AAA.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, NNN space separated integers denoting the lexicographically largest array BBB that can be formed by any rearrangement of AAA.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: The required rearrangement of array AAA is [3,2,1][3, 2, 1][3,2,1].
Test case 222: The required rearrangement of array AAA is [9,9,1,1,4][9, 9, 1, 1, 4][9,9,1,1,4].
